title: CAP Falsifiability Status Manifest
description: Machine-readable status of the seven CAP claims from 03_validation/falsifiability.md. Each claim is either executable (a gate function is provided in cap.falsifiability_gates) or deferred (it requires evidence CAP cannot produce alone, e.g. human study or longitudinal usage data). The manifest is the source of truth: test_falsifiability_gates.py reads it and runs every executable gate; any failure or any silent removal of a gated claim falsifies the corresponding claim.

claims.title: Operators are consistently assignable

evidence.threshold_description: 100% agreement among all three models on both fields for every case in the COM Grammar pack, matching the deterministic baseline.

claims.falsified_when: Any case shows disagreement among the three models on overall_verdict or primary_reading, or any model run is missing for any case.

claims.title: Transition cost predicts perceived burden
claims.evidence_required: Human cost-burden survey instrument applied to sample of analysts.
claims.blocker: No subject pool, no instrument, no longitudinal data.

claims.title: Budget-gated recommendations reduce failure rate
claims.evidence_required: Longitudinal usage data with rollback / completion outcomes.
claims.blocker: No deployed user base, no follow-up tracking.

claims.title: Telemetry signals correlate with operator failure rate
claims.evidence_required: Controlled study comparing operator success across telemetry states.
claims.blocker: Telemetry state classification of natural data is itself unvalidated.

claims.title: Adjustment Layer outperforms symptom-only repair
claims.evidence_required: Comparative study of routes from Adjustment Layer vs symptom-only baselines, with a long-term stability metric.
claims.blocker: Stability metric not yet operationalized across target domains.

claims.title: Anti-self-justification reduces dialogue drift
claims.evidence_required: Benchmarked LLM dialogue runs with self-audit on vs off, measured by hallucination rate, user friction, and trust.
claims.blocker: Benchmark suite (proxy_release_gate, model_graded_auditor) exists as scaffold but lacks human-labelled ground truth at scale.

claims.title: COM-Log format produces more useful output than free-form advice

evidence.threshold_description: The full CAP validator (JSON Schema + CAP-specific consistency checks) accepts every known-good COM-Log instance and rejects every known-bad COM-Log instance defined in the gate module.

claims.falsified_when: validator accepts a known-bad COM-Log, or rejects a known-good COM-Log, or the validator stops differentiating structured output from free-form prose.
